name: Grandma Vavricek's Kolache Recipe
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup pecan meal or finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Grated rind of one lemon
2. In a small bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water. Sprinkle with 1 tbsp sugar and let stand.
3. Scald milk in saucepan. Remove from heat and stir in butter and 1/2 cup sugar. Cool to lukewarm.
4. Add salt and egg yolks. Combine milk mixture with yeast in a large bowl.
5. Gradually add the flour until all has been used. Knead dough on lightly floured board until glossy.
6. Place in bowl, cover, and let rise in warm place (80°F) until doubled in bulk about 1 hour. This dough will not be quite as stiff as a roll dough.
7. Using a tablespoon, take egg-sized bits of the dough and roll into balls on the floured board. Place on greased baking sheet about 1 inch apart. Brush each with softened butter.
8. Let rise again, covered, until light. Make an indentation in each, large enough to hold 1 tsp of fruit filling.
9. Sprinkle fruit with some of the topping. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes. Remove from oven and brush with softened butter immediately.
10. Cool kolaches on wire rack. Makes about 3 1/2 dozen.
11. Topping: Mix all ingredients together in a mixer until the consistency of cornmeal.
12. Poppy Seed Filling: Mix well. Place by teaspoon full into kolache indentations.
13. Cream Cheese Filling: Beat all ingredients together and place by teaspoon full into indentations.
